rationale: Residence type is treated as a background location characteristic rather than an outcome of the respondent's schooling in the current design.
Sex is fixed before the respondent's schooling outcome and is used only for descriptive adjustment.
Current household income may partly reflect the respondent's own education and later-life selection, so it is not a clean pre-treatment control.
Migration-linked exposure can reflect household responses that co-evolve with schooling and later labor-market opportunities.
Current multigenerational co-residence may be shaped by education, marriage, fertility, and post-school household adjustment.
